purpose: historical vulnerability pattern transplantation into local authorized audit scope

datasets.name: Solodit
datasets.safe_use: Search historical report mechanisms; never treat search results as proof.

datasets.name: Code4rena reports
datasets.safe_use: Mine public contest report root causes and mitigations.

datasets.name: Sherlock public audits
datasets.safe_use: Use public report repositories when available.

datasets.name: Immunefi blog
datasets.safe_use: Extract economic exploit mechanisms from published incident analyses.

datasets.name: DeFiHackLabs
datasets.safe_use: Translate Foundry reproduction patterns into local-only tests.

datasets.name: SWC Registry
datasets.safe_use: Use as terminology only; the registry notes that it is no longer actively maintained.

datasets.name: SmartBugs
datasets.safe_use: Use weakness classes as static-analysis prompts.

datasets.name: SmartBugs Curated
datasets.safe_use: Use annotated examples as pattern prompts.

datasets.name: DAppSCAN
datasets.safe_use: Use project-level weakness labels as hypothesis seeds.

datasets.name: AutoMESC
datasets.safe_use: Use paper metadata and accessible samples; do not assume dataset availability.

datasets.name: ScrawlD
datasets.safe_use: Use labels as prompts for contract-specific reasoning.

datasets.name: ReEVMBench
datasets.safe_use: Use benchmark categories as coverage prompts.

datasets.name: Trail of Bits Building Secure Contracts
datasets.safe_use: Use audit methodology and invariant design guidance.

datasets.name: Local Cyfrin-derived skills
datasets.safe_use: Apply the lending, oracle, signature, precision, and access-control checklists.

datasets.name: Local Slither output
datasets.safe_use: Use warnings as cues only; require source reasoning and a local PoC.

external_bug_taxonomy.bug_shape: A capability reaches a caller or call context broader than the intended authority.
A valid signature authorizes a materially different economic effect.
Equivalent value is represented by fields that drift under ordering.
Small error crosses a later material decision threshold.
Price trust or liveness assumptions leak across a consent boundary.
Liquidation ordering realizes loss before recoverability is exhausted.
External control observes or consumes intermediate state.
A route is locally valid but violates end-user aggregate intent.
Temporary balances satisfy a check or settle twice.
Token behavior violates integration assumptions.
A claim changes economic context before all positions settle.
The same position receives inconsistent rules at a lifecycle edge.
Per-market accounting fails when custody or authorization spans markets.

external_bug_taxonomy.category: Denial-of-service that turns into value loss
external_bug_taxonomy.bug_shape: A liveness fault blocks a time-sensitive protective action.
external_bug_taxonomy.midnight_surfaces: ratifier, gate, oracle, callback, route loops

external_bug_taxonomy.bug_shape: Two fields representing one economic state update at different times.
A proof models fewer paths or identities than runtime code.
Code enforces a weaker promise than protocol intent.
external_bug_taxonomy.midnight_surfaces: bundles, offers, fee and liquidation paths

external_bug_taxonomy.bug_shape: A privileged parameter change invalidates user assumptions.
external_bug_taxonomy.midnight_surfaces: role setters, fee setter, market creator

external_bug_taxonomy.category: Griefing that escalates to economic harm
external_bug_taxonomy.bug_shape: A cheap adversarial action forces another actor into a loss-making path.
Summaries, preserved blocks, filters, or vacuity exclude a runtime composition.

historical_patterns.title: lazy borrower index update before withdrawal
first depositor or empty-market conversion discontinuity
shared custody counted twice across markets
credit and debt unit conversion ordering
liquidator-selected collateral order socializes avoidable loss

historical_patterns.title: epoch rollover reuses stale accounting context
non-escrowed maker liquidity disappears after route planning
partial fill consumes a broader group than intended
failed order leg changes fallback economics
maker callback alters later order execution
final balance used instead of route delta
multicall combines individually safe state transitions
signature reused in a different operation context

historical_patterns.title: multi-collateral price asymmetry changes seizure order
bad market oracle contaminates indirect route
gas or return-data grief after earlier effect
fee claim context changes after socialized loss
settlement fee custody overlaps another market
rounding direction differs across inverse operation
historical_patterns.source: Trail of Bits Building Secure Contracts

historical_patterns.title: one collateral liveness fault blocks basket recovery
formal alias writes one market and proves another
external call summary excludes callback effect

unexplored_equivalents.title: Duplicate-token flash-array settlement
unexplored_equivalents.sequence: Repeat one standard ERC20 in tokens[] with split amounts, overlap held custody, repay callback, and compare aggregate backing.
unexplored_equivalents.invariant: Each token leaves the call with custody unchanged after the sum of obligations.

unexplored_equivalents.title: Multicall state-transition composition
unexplored_equivalents.sequence: Batch permissionless or legitimately authorized actions that are normally separated by an observation boundary.
unexplored_equivalents.invariant: Batching cannot escape debt, collateral health, cap, or custody checks.

unexplored_equivalents.title: Liquidation-order private-gain divergence
unexplored_equivalents.sequence: Liquidate equivalent multi-collateral positions in alternate order and compare proceeds plus social loss.
unexplored_equivalents.invariant: Collateral ordering cannot create avoidable lender loss beyond documented incentives.

unexplored_equivalents.title: Lazy fee-claim context switch
unexplored_equivalents.sequence: Touch only a subset of lenders around accrued fees, bounded bad debt, claim, repay, and withdrawal.
unexplored_equivalents.invariant: Claim plus lender withdrawals remains backed and order-independent.

unexplored_equivalents.title: Partial-success poison route
unexplored_equivalents.sequence: Execute a valid route leg, then a bounded failing or gas-heavy ratifier leg, then inspect rollback or continuation.
unexplored_equivalents.invariant: Route failure cannot strand collateral or preserve partial unintended economics.

unexplored_equivalents.title: Ratified Merkle leaf economic-family collision
unexplored_equivalents.sequence: Compare signed leaves whose local fields are valid but whose group meaning crosses order families.
unexplored_equivalents.invariant: One maker-signed economic family cannot consume or unlock another without equivalent intent.

unexplored_equivalents.title: Involuntary oracle-liveness route contamination
unexplored_equivalents.sequence: Route an actor through a market whose oracle liveness later blocks a time-sensitive exit.
unexplored_equivalents.invariant: A victim cannot acquire unsafe-market exposure without explicit consent.

unexplored_equivalents.title: RCF one-wei flip after collateral-order choice
unexplored_equivalents.sequence: Place state around the RCF boundary and compare selected collateral order.
unexplored_equivalents.invariant: A tiny threshold change cannot enable non-dust private extraction.

unexplored_equivalents.title: Permit receiver-referral semantic split
unexplored_equivalents.sequence: Use valid pull authority while varying receiver and referral in an economically distinct route.
unexplored_equivalents.invariant: Token-pull consent cannot authorize an unbound economic beneficiary.

unexplored_equivalents.title: Read-only intermediate-state route influence
unexplored_equivalents.sequence: Observe intermediate state during one leg and use it to select or affect a later leg.
unexplored_equivalents.invariant: Intermediate observations cannot create an unbacked later settlement.

unexplored_equivalents.title: Flash empty-array callback as route primitive
unexplored_equivalents.sequence: Invoke callback with empty arrays only as a component of a later state transition.
unexplored_equivalents.invariant: Empty temporary-liquidity calls cannot bypass an unrelated invariant.

unexplored_equivalents.title: Force-approve residue under standard token
unexplored_equivalents.sequence: Execute repeated standard-token routes and inspect allowances plus token custody.
unexplored_equivalents.invariant: Residual allowance cannot move caller funds outside caller-authorized pulls.

unexplored_equivalents.title: ERC2612 tolerate-front-run semantic change
unexplored_equivalents.sequence: Front-run permit consumption, retain allowance, and execute a different route context.
unexplored_equivalents.invariant: Tolerance cannot widen the economic intent beyond the allowance owner decision.

unexplored_equivalents.title: Gate liveness plus maturity exit loss
unexplored_equivalents.sequence: Combine a route-acquired market position, gate liveness change, and maturity boundary.
unexplored_equivalents.invariant: A third party cannot force time-sensitive loss through indirect gate exposure.

unexplored_equivalents.title: Multi-market aggregate custody after nested callback
unexplored_equivalents.sequence: Use standard shared token markets and a later callback state action, then reconcile aggregate custody.
unexplored_equivalents.invariant: Per-market local settlement composes into aggregate token backing.

unexplored_equivalents.title: Settlement fee claim around shared custody
unexplored_equivalents.sequence: Accrue and claim fees while another market uses the same standard token and compare custody.
unexplored_equivalents.invariant: Claims cannot spend backing attributable to another market.

unexplored_equivalents.title: Maker liquidity disappearance after partial route
unexplored_equivalents.sequence: Build multiple-leg route where non-escrowed maker balance vanishes after an earlier valid leg.
unexplored_equivalents.invariant: User aggregate bounds and atomicity hold despite disappearing maker liquidity.

unexplored_equivalents.title: Root replacement changes fallback economics
unexplored_equivalents.sequence: Consume partially, cancel root, activate a semantically different root, execute prepared route.
unexplored_equivalents.invariant: Prepared route cannot violate explicit user bounds after root lifecycle change.

unexplored_equivalents.title: Repeated collateral index activation order
unexplored_equivalents.sequence: Activate and zero multiple indexes in differing orders before liquidation.
unexplored_equivalents.invariant: Bitmap and balances select the same collateral set.

unexplored_equivalents.title: Index boundary at maximum collateral length
unexplored_equivalents.sequence: Exercise highest valid collateral index with alternate activation order.
unexplored_equivalents.invariant: Collateral index arithmetic cannot address the wrong asset.

unexplored_equivalents.title: Zero-withdraw bitmap transition as composition component
unexplored_equivalents.sequence: Issue zero withdrawal before later oracle/gate and liquidation actions.
unexplored_equivalents.invariant: Zero-value calls cannot alter later non-dust decisions.

unexplored_equivalents.title: Continuous-fee threshold flips health decision
unexplored_equivalents.sequence: Accrue exactly around fee rounding boundary before health-sensitive action.
unexplored_equivalents.invariant: Dust cannot trigger material collateral retention or escape.

unexplored_equivalents.title: Tick conversion one-wei liquidation branch
unexplored_equivalents.sequence: Set prices around tick conversion and follow through liquidation.
unexplored_equivalents.invariant: Precision remainder cannot create non-dust social loss.

unexplored_equivalents.title: Route quote survives maturity transition
unexplored_equivalents.sequence: Build route before maturity and execute at boundary with fallback offers.
unexplored_equivalents.invariant: Lifecycle change cannot silently widen user economics.

unexplored_equivalents.title: Relayer combines signed authorization and referral
unexplored_equivalents.sequence: Use an authorized relayer with a valid grant and substitute route beneficiary fields.
unexplored_equivalents.invariant: Grant semantics remain bounded to user-authorized economic effects.

unexplored_equivalents.title: Ratifier return-data grief after earlier route effect
unexplored_equivalents.sequence: Use bounded oversized return or revert data in a later route leg.
unexplored_equivalents.invariant: A hostile leg cannot preserve earlier unintended state or force third-party loss.

unexplored_equivalents.title: Oracle return-data grief during protective action
unexplored_equivalents.sequence: Use bounded oracle failure only after indirect route exposure.
unexplored_equivalents.invariant: Liveness fault cannot become involuntary solvency loss.

unexplored_equivalents.title: Callback later-leg gas grief
unexplored_equivalents.sequence: Spend bounded gas after one successful route leg and inspect transaction atomicity.
unexplored_equivalents.invariant: Callback grief cannot strand route-owned value.

unexplored_equivalents.title: Consumed semantic collision across market IDs
unexplored_equivalents.sequence: Reuse group identifier across markets with distinct economic meaning.
unexplored_equivalents.invariant: Consumed accounting cannot cross maker intent boundaries unexpectedly.

unexplored_equivalents.title: Formal external-call summary excludes route state
unexplored_equivalents.sequence: Map unresolved calls and unmodelled periphery into an executable harness plan.
unexplored_equivalents.invariant: Formal assurance gaps require runtime reproduction before severity promotion.

unexplored_equivalents.title: Event reconstruction misses lazy position settlement
unexplored_equivalents.sequence: Replay emitted events around lazy fee and loss updates and compare storage.
unexplored_equivalents.invariant: Indexers can reconstruct economically relevant state or docs identify limitation.

unexplored_equivalents.title: Claimable fee zero-to-nonzero discontinuity
unexplored_equivalents.sequence: Cross minimum claim threshold then continue into lender withdrawal.
unexplored_equivalents.invariant: Claim threshold cannot shift a material liability.

unexplored_equivalents.title: Withdrawal equality branch after loss update
unexplored_equivalents.sequence: Place lender claim one wei around withdrawable equality after bounded social loss.
unexplored_equivalents.invariant: Equality boundary cannot externalize another lender's backing.

unexplored_equivalents.title: Non-escrowed maker balance race plus exact route
unexplored_equivalents.sequence: Remove maker balance between quote and route while preserving alternate offers.
unexplored_equivalents.invariant: Exact-route bounds remain enforced under liquidity race.

unexplored_equivalents.title: Market creator plus route-builder consent mismatch
unexplored_equivalents.sequence: Place a malicious quality market into mixed route and prove whether user consent is explicit.
unexplored_equivalents.invariant: Permissionless creation cannot contaminate an unrelated user position.

unexplored_equivalents.title: Multi-collateral oracle asymmetry after partial repay
unexplored_equivalents.sequence: Partially repay before asymmetric oracle liveness and order-selective liquidation.
unexplored_equivalents.invariant: Repay cannot worsen recoverability through order-dependent accounting.

unexplored_equivalents.title: Flash duplicate array plus multicall nesting
unexplored_equivalents.sequence: Use repeated tokens and callback multicall without direct repayment bypass assumptions.
unexplored_equivalents.invariant: Nested standard-token settlement remains aggregate-backed.

unexplored_equivalents.title: Ratified root same-leaf duplicate proof
unexplored_equivalents.sequence: Test duplicated proof elements and repeated leaf consumption as economic-family component.
unexplored_equivalents.invariant: Proof structure cannot widen fill authority.

unexplored_equivalents.title: Maturity quote plus authorization revocation ordering
unexplored_equivalents.sequence: Prepare route, change delegation, cross maturity, and execute.
unexplored_equivalents.invariant: Authorization and lifecycle checks bind at settlement.

unexplored_equivalents.title: LossFactor control execution with all positions touched
unexplored_equivalents.sequence: Compare adversarial touch order with eager-touch control.
unexplored_equivalents.invariant: Lazy updates cannot change aggregate redemption value.

lender claim changes economic context before peers settle

liquidator choice changes social loss rather than only incentive
fee schedule context changes between route construction and settlement

poc_backlog.title: Duplicate-token flash-array settlement
poc_backlog.actors: permissionless caller and local repayment callback
poc_backlog.sequence: Repeat one standard token in `tokens[]`, vary split amounts and order, overlap protocol-held custody, and compare against one aggregated loan.
poc_backlog.assertion: Custody after callback equals custody before callback for each token and liabilities remain backed.

poc_backlog.title: Multicall state-transition composition
poc_backlog.functions: multicall plus public or legitimately authorized core methods
poc_backlog.sequence: Batch state transitions that normally occur across transactions and compare with sequential execution.
poc_backlog.assertion: No batching-only debt escape, collateral escape, cap bypass, or third-party transfer.

poc_backlog.title: Liquidation-order social-loss divergence
poc_backlog.sequence: Create equivalent multi-collateral positions, liquidate collateral indexes in alternate orders, and reconcile private proceeds plus social loss.
poc_backlog.assertion: Equivalent initial positions cannot create avoidable lender loss solely from attacker-selected order.

poc_backlog.title: Continuous-fee claim context switch
poc_backlog.sequence: Accrue fees, touch lender subsets, create bounded loss, claim, withdraw, and compare eager-touch control.
poc_backlog.assertion: Aggregate lender payouts plus fee claim remain backed and control-equivalent.

poc_backlog.title: Partial-success poison route
poc_backlog.source_pattern: aggregator earlier-effect plus hostile later leg
poc_backlog.actors: route caller and local hostile ratifier
poc_backlog.sequence: Execute a valid leg before a bounded failing or gas-heavy leg and inspect atomicity or continuation.
poc_backlog.assertion: No stranded collateral or preserved unintended partial economics.

poc_backlog.title: Ratified family semantic collision
poc_backlog.sequence: Construct signed roots and groups with distinct economic family expectations.
poc_backlog.assertion: One family cannot consume or unlock another without equivalent maker intent.

poc_backlog.title: Involuntary oracle-route contamination
poc_backlog.sequence: Mix a bad-liveness market into route execution and prove whether victim consent is explicit.
poc_backlog.assertion: No unchosen market exposure can force a later loss.

poc_backlog.title: RCF threshold collateral-order flip
poc_backlog.sequence: Place state around RCF and compare collateral order with a one-wei perturbation.
poc_backlog.assertion: One wei cannot trigger non-dust avoidable lender loss.

poc_backlog.title: Permit receiver-referral split
poc_backlog.actors: token owner, relayer, receiver, referral recipient
poc_backlog.sequence: Hold pull authority constant while changing route beneficiary fields.
poc_backlog.assertion: Signed token pull cannot fund an unbound economic beneficiary.

poc_backlog.title: Read-only intermediate-state route influence
poc_backlog.sequence: Observe intermediate state in one leg and use it to alter a later leg.
poc_backlog.assertion: Later settlement remains backed and within user bounds.

poc_backlog.title: Standard-token approval residue sequence
poc_backlog.sequence: Run repeated standard-token routes and inspect allowances plus custody.
poc_backlog.assertion: No later route can move funds beyond fresh caller-authorized pull.

poc_backlog.title: ERC2612 tolerate-front-run context switch
poc_backlog.sequence: Consume permit first, leave allowance, then execute changed route.
poc_backlog.assertion: Fallback allowance cannot widen economic intent.

poc_backlog.title: Gate liveness maturity escalation
poc_backlog.sequence: Acquire position indirectly, change gate behavior, cross maturity, attempt exit and liquidation.
poc_backlog.assertion: Indirect exposure cannot force avoidable non-dust loss.

poc_backlog.title: Shared-token nested callback aggregate custody
poc_backlog.sequence: Use shared loan token markets and a nested backed state action.
poc_backlog.assertion: Aggregate custody covers aggregate liabilities.

poc_backlog.title: Maker-liquidity disappearance exact route
poc_backlog.sequence: Remove maker liquidity after route planning and rely on fallback legs.
poc_backlog.assertion: Exact bounds hold or transaction reverts atomically.

poc_backlog.title: Root replacement fallback economics
poc_backlog.sequence: Partially consume, cancel, re-ratify different root, and execute prepared route.
poc_backlog.assertion: Prepared route cannot exceed explicit user bounds.

poc_backlog.title: Collateral index activation permutation
poc_backlog.sequence: Activate and zero collateral indexes in permutations before liquidation.
poc_backlog.assertion: Bitmap-selected set equals nonzero balance set.

poc_backlog.title: Maximum collateral index boundary
poc_backlog.sequence: Exercise the highest valid collateral index and liquidation.
poc_backlog.assertion: Selected collateral is the intended array member.

poc_backlog.title: Continuous-fee threshold health flip
poc_backlog.sequence: Accrue one wei around fee threshold before health-sensitive exit.
poc_backlog.assertion: Dust cannot enable material collateral escape or forced retention.

poc_backlog.title: Tick threshold liquidation branch
poc_backlog.sequence: Exercise adjacent ticks then follow lifecycle into liquidation.
poc_backlog.assertion: Precision difference cannot externalize non-dust loss.

poc_backlog.title: Maturity quote route lifecycle
poc_backlog.sequence: Build fallback route before maturity and execute at the exact boundary.
poc_backlog.assertion: Bounds hold or route reverts atomically.

poc_backlog.title: Relayer authorization referral composition
poc_backlog.sequence: Use valid authorization while varying referral and receiver.
poc_backlog.assertion: No unbound beneficiary receives user-funded value.

poc_backlog.title: Ratifier return-data grief after valid leg
poc_backlog.sequence: Use bounded large return or revert data after an earlier valid leg.
poc_backlog.assertion: Failure rolls back or cannot force third-party economic loss.

poc_backlog.title: Event-storage reconstruction mismatch
poc_backlog.sequence: Replay events around lazy loss and fee settlement and compare storage.
poc_backlog.assertion: Any reconstruction gap is documented; promote only with concrete economic escalation.

poc_backlog.title: Lazy-loss eager-touch control
poc_backlog.sequence: Compare eager-touch control with adversarial subset-touch ordering.
poc_backlog.assertion: Aggregate redemption value is ordering-independent.
